extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked solo with 50km remaining after an earlier crash, building a 90-second gap that even ALAPHILIPPE and ASGREEN couldn't close despite late surges.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked immediately after recovering from crash, exploiting psychological assumption he'd be weakened to prevent organized chase.
ALAPHILIPPE expended critical energy bridging post-crash, rendering him unable to respond when POGAČAR launched with 50km remaining.
ASGREEN waited until third-to-last sector to lead chase, far too late given 90-second deficit already established.
Valverde's second place at age 41 signals superior sector-specific pacing versus younger explosive riders.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: crashed early with MATTHEWS and others, yet soloed from 50km out suggesting crash damage assessment errors by rivals
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: bridging effort post-crash depleted reserves needed for final 50km, poor energy management for strato finish
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: neutral: led late chase on sector three but initiated pursuit only after 90-second gap established
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: abandoned after early crash despite initially appearing capable of continuing
rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Carlos: neutral: briefly followed POGAČAR's move but isolated without team support, drifted back before final 25km
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: second place at 41 years signals superior sector pacing strategy over younger climbers
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: top-ten finish after following ASGREEN's late chase suggests emerging strato capability
